statement_lock.original_statement:
Is it true that, for every integer $t\geq 1$, there is some integer $a$ such that\[\binom{n}{k}=a\](with $1\leq k\leq n/2$) has exactly $t$ solutions?




Erd\H{o}s \cite{Er96b} credits this to himself and Gordon 'many years ago', but it is more commonly known as Singmaster's conjecture . For $t=3$ one could take $a=120$, and for $t=4$ one could take $a=3003$. There are no known examples for $t\geq 5$. Both Erd\H{o}s and Singmaster believed the answer to this question is no, and in fact that there exists an absolute upper bound on the number of solutions. Matomäki, Radziwill, Shao, Tao, and Teräväinen \cite{MRSTT22} have proved that there are always at most two solutions if we restrict $k$ to\[k\geq \exp((\log n)^{2/3+\epsilon}),\]assuming $a$ is sufficiently large depending on $\epsilon>0$.




References

[Er96b] Erd\H{o}s, Paul, Some problems I presented or planned to present in my short talk . Analytic number theory, Vol. 1 (Allerton Park, IL, 1995) (1996), 333-335.

[MRSTT22] Matom\"{a}ki, Kaisa and Radziwi\l\l, Maksym and Shao, Xuancheng and Tao, Terence and Ter\"{a}v\"{a}inen, Joni, Singmaster's conjecture in the interior of {P}ascal's triangle . Q. J. Math. (2022), 1137--1177.
acceptance_criteria: Prove or disprove the exact original statement without changing quantifiers.
Do not add hypotheses, restrict domains, weaken conclusions, or omit subproblems.
Resolve every boundary case and every explicitly numbered task.
Treat ambiguity as a blocking defect rather than choosing a convenient interpretation.

subgoal_graph.summary:
Status: unresolved. The reports agree on several local discrete lemmas but disagree on the domain, slope orientation, and strength of the finite certificates; agreement is therefore not treated as proof. Ranked portfolio: (1) the negative t=5 compatibility route has the best mathematical fit and highest information gain, because excluding exactly four interior representations would immediately disprove the universal statement; its decisive gaps are LOW_HIGH_EXCLUSION and HIGH_EXTENSION_BOUND. (2) the displacement-word route is highly falsifiable and may construct or exclude four-point collision chains, but it cannot become global without both an effective variable bound and a bound on the displacement complexity of a minimal witness. (3) the Pell multi-lift route has a verified pair-collision seed but presently lacks both scalable independent lifts and uniform exclusion of unintended layers. Arithmetic invariants and bounded computation remain supporting filters only. Every discrete branch is quarantined behind DOMAIN; if the immutable wording does not uniquely determine integer n and k, the process must terminate with a formal ambiguity certificate rather than a conditional answer. Source provenance only: 

Pasted text

subgoals.claim: Determine from the immutable wording whether n and k are uniquely required to be integers in the standard combinatorial domain. If this cannot be established without adding a convention, issue a formal ambiguity certificate and halt all discrete proof branches.

Conditional on DOMAIN establishing the discrete interpretation, define D={(n,k) in Z^2: n>=2 and 1<=k<=floor(n/2)} and verify all basic monotonicity statements using the correctly oriented quotients C(n+1,k)/C(n,k)=(n+1)/(n+1-k)>1 and C(n,k+1)/C(n,k)=(n-k)/(k+1)>1 whenever k+1<=n/2.
Verify explicitly that C(2k,k)>=2^k for every integer k>=1 and that C(2k+2,k+1)/C(2k,k)=2(2k+1)/(k+1)>1 for every integer k>=0, with the initial cases checked separately.

For integer a define M(a)=#{(n,k) in D:C(n,k)=a}. Prove M(a)=0 for a<=1 and, for a>=2, M(a)=1+mu(a), where S_k={C(n,k):n in Z and n>=2k} and mu(a)=#{k>=2:a in S_k}. Also prove each fiber is finite and k<=floor(log_2 a).
Certify with complete exclusion of all omitted layers that M(2)=1, M(6)=2, M(120)=3, and M(3003)=4; hence t=5 is the first possible failure under the discrete interpretation.
Prove that if C(n,k)=C(m,l), k<l, and both vertices are in D, then n>m. Consequently every interior fiber is a chain with strictly increasing lower indices and strictly decreasing row indices.

Let epsilon_j(a)=1 when a is in S_j and 0 otherwise for j=2,3,4, and let h(a)=#{k>=5:a in S_k}. For the exact case mu(a)=4, prove the exhaustive partition: h=1 with all three low layers present; h=2 with exactly two low layers present; h=3 with exactly one low layer present; or h=4 with no low layer present. State separately the weaker inequalities applicable when mu(a)>=4.

For admissible vertices (n,k) and (n-d,k+e), with integers d,e>=1 and n-d>=2(k+e), prove that C(n,k)=C(n-d,k+e) is equivalent to n^{under d}(k+1)^{over e}=(n-k)^{under(d+e)}, where all falling and rising factorials and their ranges are explicitly defined.

Resolve the contradictory scout claims about collision slopes by a complete edge-sum proof. With d_i=n_i-n_{i+1} and e_i=k_{i+1}-k_i, determine and prove the correct strict orientation, checking it against the 3003 chain, whose ratios e_i/d_i are 1/21 and 1.
For every discrete representation C(n,k)=a, prove that n/gcd(n,k) divides a, stating and checking the coprimality hypotheses used in Euclid's lemma.
For every prime p prove v_p(C(n,k))=sum_{j>=1}(floor(n/p^j)-floor(k/p^j)-floor((n-k)/p^j)), and separately verify the equivalent carry-count interpretation.

If equal coefficients are represented at (n_i,k_i) and at the smallest-row vertex (m,l), prove that the numerator block n_i(n_i-1)...(n_i-k_i+1) divides k_i!m!, and hence every prime dividing that block is at most m.
For a prime p>n/2 and an admissible vertex (n,k), prove p divides C(n,k) exactly when n-k<p<=n. Determine whether several ordered representations force incompatible upper-prime shadows.
Prove or disprove by an explicit counterexample the proposed assertion that two equal binomial coefficients with the same reduced denominator n/gcd(n,k) must arise from the same vertex.

Independently reproduce or refute, using exact integer arithmetic and a fully specified exhaustive enumeration, the bounded statement M(a)<=4 for every integer 2<=a<=10^30. Record this only as a bounded certificate and candidate-generation tool; no global node may infer an unbounded conclusion from it.

Derive the exact admissible integer system for a in S_2 intersect S_3 intersect S_4: 3(X^2-1)=4(Y^3-Y) and 48(X^2-1)=(Z^2-1)(Z^2-9), with X>=7 odd, Y>=5, and Z>=13 odd, and prove the equivalence in both directions.
As a sufficient obligation for the selected negative strategy, prove that S_2 intersect S_3 intersect S_4 intersect S_k is empty for every integer k>=5, or produce a counterexample.
As the second sufficient obligation for the selected negative strategy, prove that h(a)>=2 implies mu(a)<=3 for every integer a, or produce a value violating this implication.

Combine T5_CASE_SPLIT, LOW_HIGH_EXCLUSION, and HIGH_EXTENSION_BOUND to prove that no integer a has mu(a)=4, equivalently no integer a has exactly five discrete solutions. This node remains open unless both sufficient obligations are proved globally.

For a displacement word W=((d_1,e_1),...,(d_r,e_r)), define every cumulative vertex n_i=n_1-sum_{j<i}d_j and k_i=k_1+sum_{j<i}e_j. State the full equations n_i^{under d_i}(k_i+1)^{over e_i}=(n_i-k_i)^{under(d_i+e_i)} and impose n_i>=2k_i at every vertex, including the terminal endpoint.

Before any purported exhaustive word search, prove for each fixed word W either an effective bound on all admissible integer starts (n_1,k_1) or a complete decision procedure applicable to the resulting polynomial system.

Prove an effective absolute bound on the displacement complexity sum_i(d_i+e_i) of a minimal four-interior-representation witness, or establish another finite reduction that covers every possible such witness.

Only after WORD_VARIABLE_BOUND and WORD_WITNESS_BOUND are proved, exhaust all permitted displacement words and starts, obtaining either a certified four-point interior collision or a certified exclusion of all such chains.
For every collision value produced by WORD_ENUMERATION, inspect all layers 2<=k<=floor(log_2 a) and prove its exact interior multiplicity, rather than merely the number of displayed vertices.
Construct, for every q>=0, a uniformly solvable family of admissible displacement words yielding exactly q interior representations, together with a uniform proof excluding every unintended layer.
Verify independently that the Pell-type recurrence generates infinitely many admissible adjacent collisions C(n,k)=C(n-1,k+1), while making no assertion about additional representations.

Determine whether some Pell-pair value has two additional independent interior representations, thereby producing four displayed interior layers; the first checkpoint is simultaneous satisfaction of the triangular square condition and one further layer equation.
Develop a theorem that, for arbitrarily large q, forces one Pell-derived value to lie on q distinct prescribed interior layers. Pair collisions alone do not satisfy this node.
For the multi-lift family, prove uniformly that no unprescribed layer S_k contains the constructed value; finite checking of individual terms is insufficient.
Use PELL_MULTI_LIFT and PELL_EXACTNESS to realize every prescribed exact interior multiplicity q>=0, including the boundary case q=0.

Compare the displacement-word and Pell branches and extract at least one genuinely scalable operation that realizes every exact interior multiplicity; failure or bounded success of one branch must not be presented as success of the other.

Conditional on EXACT_MULTIPLICITY_SCALING, prove that for every integer t>=1 there exists an integer a with exactly t discrete solutions by taking q=t-1 and verifying all boundary and exactness conditions.

Prove or disprove the exact immutable statement without changing its quantifiers or domain. A valid terminal result may come from NEGATIVE_T5 or POSITIVE_RESOLUTION only after DOMAIN is resolved; otherwise return the formal ambiguity certificate. No solution is presently claimed.

regulator.rationale:
The rejection is not repairable by local proof edits alone. The current DAG contains an omitted DOMAIN dependency, an unvalidated slope node, a false finite-decidability inference for bounded displacement words, an uncertified computational node with an incorrectly printed recurrence, an under-specified high-extension theorem, a missing Pell-family dependency, and two conjunctive dependency lists that should be disjunctive. Nevertheless, the conditional normalization, exact multiplicities for t=1,2,3,4, fiber ordering, displacement identity, adjacent-layer Pell family, and bounded exclusion through 10^30 can be retained after rechecking. Thus the approach family is not exhausted, making REVISE_PLAN cheaper than REWRITE. The uploaded candidate is identified only as the audited artifact, not as mathematical evidence: 

Pasted text
new_constraints: Make DOMAIN a hard prerequisite of every discrete mathematical node. Under the immutable acceptance criterion, conditional work with integer n and k must be explicitly quarantined and cannot discharge GOAL.

Add explicit nodes for fixed-layer monotonicity, half-row monotonicity, central-binomial growth, fiber ordering, and the adjacent-layer Pell family; attach all claims that use them to those dependencies.

Reset SLOPE_AUDIT to unproved. Any replacement proof must consistently establish d_i/e_i strictly decreasing, equivalently e_i/d_i strictly increasing, and must check the 3003 chain where the ratios are 21,1 and 1/21,1 respectively.

Delete the assertion that finitely many displacement words imply finite exact decidability. WORD_ENUMERATION may be restored only after proving an effective bound on n and k for each word class or supplying a complete applicable decision procedure.

Do not allow WORD_COLLISION to depend on WORD_ENUMERATION until that enumeration node is repaired; polynomial equations must also include admissibility, positivity, ordering, and distinctness constraints.

For SEARCH_CERT use the correct recurrence binom(n+1,k)=binom(n,k)(n+1)/(n+1-k), impose n>=2k in every low-layer membership test, and require reproducible code or a complete certificate. Treat the result as finite-range evidence only.
Do not infer any global exclusion from the computation through 10^30.

Treat LOW_HIGH_EXCLUSION and HIGH_EXTENSION_BOUND as separate major bottlenecks. SLOPE_AUDIT alone is not an adequate dependency for HIGH_EXTENSION_BOUND; a new global compatibility ingredient is required.
Add a proved PELL_ADJACENT_FAMILY node before PELL_DOUBLE_LIFT. No lifting or exactness claim may assume that family implicitly.
Represent alternative branches explicitly: POSITIVE_SCALING follows from WORD_SCALING OR PELL_SCALING, and final resolution requires DOMAIN together with NEGATIVE_T5 OR POSITIVE_SCALING.
A value of multiplicity five, or unbounded multiplicity, is not sufficient for the positive universal statement; exact multiplicity must be constructed for every positive integer t.
Do not reuse the candidate's displayed quotient, slope, or recurrence formulas without fresh derivation and boundary checks.

regulator.rationale:
The failure is structural, not merely a collection of local execution defects. Several local claims are repairable, including the displacement identity, slope proof, divisor lemma, and word equations, but repairing them would still leave both terminal routes entirely open. DOMAIN remains a blocking ambiguity; T5_CASE_SPLIT is misstated and confuses sufficient branch-killing conditions with necessary conditions; ARITHMETIC_INVARIANTS is only partially supplied; WORD_SYSTEM omits essential equations and endpoint admissibility; and neither GLOBAL_COMPATIBILITY nor any scalable exact construction exists. The sound monotonicity, normalization, low-multiplicity, Pell-pair, low-layer-algebra, and bounded-computation components should be retained, so a full rewrite is unnecessarily expensive. Input provenance only: 

Pasted text
new_constraints: Keep every discrete claim quarantined until DOMAIN is resolved from the immutable wording; do not substitute the semidiscrete theorem for the original statement.
If DOMAIN cannot be uniquely resolved, stop the proof search at a formal ambiguity certificate rather than claiming a conditional answer resolves GOAL.
Retain only locally verified lemmas after rewriting every malformed definition, equation, inequality, summation range, multiplication sign, and boundary condition explicitly.
Restrict central-row strict growth to the proved range and separately check all initial parity transitions.
State T5_CASE_SPLIT with well-typed layer sets S_k={C(n,k): n is an integer and n>=2k}; distinguish the exact case mu(a)=4 from the broader case mu(a)>=4.
Do not describe LOW_HIGH_EXCLUSION and HIGH_EXTENSION_BOUND as logically necessary for every possible t=5 proof; identify them only as sufficient obligations for the selected negative strategy.

Replace WORD_SYSTEM by the full edge equations n_i^{under d_i}(k_i+1)^{over e_i}=(n_i-k_i)^{under(d_i+e_i)} after cumulative shifts, and impose admissibility at every vertex, including the terminal endpoint.

Split ARITHMETIC_INVARIANTS into separately checkable nodes for reduced-denominator divisibility, valuation/carry identities, numerator-block smoothness, prime-shadow consequences, and counterexamples to injectivity.
Do not activate WORD_ENUMERATION until an effective variable bound or a complete applicable decision procedure is proved; finiteness of the word list alone is insufficient.
Treat the Pell recurrence only as a source of adjacent two-layer collisions until an independent multi-lift theorem and a uniform exactness theorem are proved.
Treat SEARCH_CERT solely as the bounded statement M(a)<=4 for a<=10^30; no global dependency may use it without a proved finite-to-infinite principle.

Require a genuinely new global ingredient before continuing the negative route: either close the low-layer/high-layer Diophantine system or prove a simultaneous compatibility theorem for all representations of one value.
Require a genuinely new scalable operation before continuing the positive route: it must realize every exact interior multiplicity and uniformly exclude unintended layers.
Do not discharge NEGATIVE_T5, POSITIVE_SCALING, or GOAL by acknowledgment of remaining gaps, finite testing, heuristic scarcity, or repaired local geometry.

The stated monotonicities of U and G are also reversed, and the arithmetic assertion 3/63=21 is false.'}

failed_approaches.reusable_lesson: Do not repeat this proof path without a new ingredient that resolves every listed obstruction.

obstructions: CENTRAL_GROWTH is stated without a sufficient range qualification; R_0=R_1=1 contradicts unqualified strict growth.
Claim 1 changes or leaves ambiguous the domain and defines D_Z inconsistently with all later uses of the automatic k=1 representation.
Claim 10 does not correctly define S_j or h(a): its displayed set-builder notation lacks the quantified row variable and treats k as belonging to S_k rather than a as belonging to S_k.
Claim 11's derivation is missing one side of the coefficient equality in the displayed proof.
Claim 12 contains missing comparison signs and omits the left side of the logarithmic sum identity. Repairing these omissions is prohibited in an adversarial statement-integrity review.
Claim 15 contains the malformed equation 'p,a=q...' where multiplication is required.
Claim 15 uses Euclid's lemma and the least-common-multiple divisibility property without stating their exact hypotheses.
Claim 17 also omits admissibility of the final endpoint, and the collision (15,5) to (14,8) is an explicit spurious solution admitted by the stated constraints.
Claim 17 omits the actual polynomial equations it claims to derive.
Claim 18 relies on unspecified correctness properties of the programming language's exact combinatorial, square-root, integer-arithmetic, and heap primitives.
Claim 2 implicitly uses the Intermediate Value Theorem and a range theorem for continuous strictly increasing unbounded functions without stating the theorem or fully checking its hypotheses.
Claim 2 proves a theorem only after introducing a generalized real-variable binomial coefficient absent from the immutable statement.
Claim 5 refers to the displayed central coefficient as the row maximum without stating and checking binomial symmetry and the required monotonicity on the complementary half-row.
Claim 9 uses asymptotic facts involving logarithms versus positive powers without stating the required limiting results.
Consequently, no global exclusion of exactly five solutions is established.
DIVISOR_INVARIANT contains a malformed central identity where multiplication is required.
Fatal acceptance failure: the candidate expressly concludes that neither terminal route is completed, so it does not prove or disprove the exact original statement.
No arbitrary Pell multi-lift or uniform exclusion of unintended layers is supplied.
No construction realizes every exact interior multiplicity q at least zero.
No construction realizes every exact interior multiplicity q.
No effective bound or complete decision procedure is supplied for scalable displacement-word systems.
No global compatibility theorem proves that two high-layer representations force total interior multiplicity at most three.
No global compatibility theorem shows that two high-layer representations force total interior multiplicity at most three.
No global exclusion of simultaneous membership in layers 2, 3, 4 and a layer k at least 5 is proved.
No uniform exactness framework is established for any infinite candidate family.

SLOPE_AUDIT omits the left-hand logarithmic sum, summation ranges, comparison signs, and a decisive strict inequality. Its intended proof may be reconstructible, but the submitted proof is incomplete.
T5_SPLIT proves its dichotomy only under M(a)=5, while the validated node requests the corresponding statement for every mu(a) at least 4.
T5_SPLIT treats two strong branch-killing statements as necessary conditions, although they are only sufficient for its proposed negative strategy.
The Pell branch supplies adjacent pairs but no arbitrary compatible multi-lifts and no uniform exclusion of unintended representations.
The assertion that all 19 claims were checked overstates the status of several syntactically incomplete claims.
The candidate does not exclude multiplicity five under the discrete interpretation.
The candidate explicitly reaches no proof or disproof of the immutable universal statement, so it fails the primary acceptance criterion.
The definitions of S_j and h(a) in T5_SPLIT are ill-typed and omit necessary quantified membership conditions.
The derivation of DISPLACEMENT_IDENTITY omits the complete starting equality and is not a finished proof as written.
The displacement-word branch lacks a complete polynomial system, an effective variable bound, and a complete decision procedure.
The displayed definition of the discrete domain is malformed and can be read as requiring k at least 2, contradicting the candidate's repeated use of the automatic k=1 representation.
The finite computation covers only a at most 10^30 and has no proved finite-to-infinite extension.
The finite computation stops at a equal to 10^30 and has no finite-to-infinite extension.
The global exclusion of simultaneous membership in layers 2, 3, 4, and some layer k at least 5 is unproved.

The immutable statement does not explicitly quantify the domains of n and k, and the candidate does not establish a unique interpretation. Its discrete and semidiscrete interpretations yield different solution counts.
The immutable statement's domain for n and k remains unresolved. Consequently, no conditional discrete or semidiscrete result proves or disproves the exact statement.
The reproducible finite certificate establishes only the bounded conclusion M(a) at most 4 for a at most 10^30 and cannot discharge either global resolution route.
The semidiscrete positive result changes the problem by introducing a real upper argument and a generalized binomial coefficient not specified in the immutable statement.
The valid finite certificate and local lemmas cannot substitute for either missing terminal route.
The validated arithmetic-invariants subgoal is only partially addressed; valuation, carry, smoothness, prime-shadow, and failed-injectivity components are absent.
Under the intended discrete interpretation, neither a proof nor a disproof of the universal statement is supplied.
WORD_SYSTEM also omits terminal half-row admissibility; for example, the formally admitted edge from (15,5) to (14,8) satisfies the collision equality but has an inadmissible endpoint.
WORD_SYSTEM omits the left-hand side and equality of every asserted displacement equation, so the promised polynomial system is not actually stated.
